# Libraries & Pre-Processing

```{r Libraies and Pre-Processing}
# Load necessary libraries
library(glmnet)
library(tidymodels)
library(dplyr)
library(parsnip)
library(readr) # For write_csv()
 
# 1. Load data and preserve original IDs
sp_train_original <- read.csv('C:\\Users\\jasmi\\OneDrive - Southern Methodist University\\6302\\Data Assignments\\DA1\\train.csv')
sp_test_original <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\jasmi\\OneDrive - Southern Methodist University\\6302\\Data Assignments\\DA1\\test.csv")
 
 
# 2. Separate IDs and data for preprocessing
sp_train_ids <- sp_train_original$Id # Store training IDs
sp_test_ids <- sp_test_original$Id # Store test IDs
 
sp_train <- sp_train_original[, -1]  # Remove ID column for training data
sp_test <- sp_test_original[, -1] # Remove ID column for test data
 
# Removing outliers
rows_to_remove <- c(524, 1299)
sp_train <- sp_train_original[-rows_to_remove, ]
 
# 3. Create sp_trainY (outcome variable)
sp_trainY <- sp_train$SalePrice
 
# 4. Combine training and test data *before* factor conversion
combined_data <- bind_rows(sp_train, sp_test)
 
# 5. Convert character columns to factors in the COMBINED data
combined_data <- combined_data %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), as.factor))
 
# 6. Split back into training and test sets
sp_train <- combined_data %>% filter(row_number() <= nrow(sp_train))
sp_test <- combined_data %>% filter(row_number() > nrow(sp_train))
 
# 7. Separate predictors and outcome in training data
sp_trainX <- sp_train[, -which(names(sp_train) == "SalePrice")] # All columns except SalePrice
 
# 8. Create and PREPARE the recipe using the COMBINED data (but fit on training only)
sp_recipe <- recipe(SalePrice ~ ., data = sp_train) %>% # Recipe uses training data
  step_rm(Utilities) %>%
  step_impute_knn(all_predictors()) %>%
  step_novel(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_other(all_nominal_predictors(), threshold = 0.05) %>%
  ##########PART 1##########
  step_mutate(
    TotalArea = TotalBsmtSF + X1stFlrSF + X2ndFlrSF + GarageArea + OpenPorchSF + WoodDeckSF + ScreenPorch + PoolArea, 
    Total_Bathrooms = FullBath + 0.5 * HalfBath + BsmtFullBath + 0.5 * BsmtHalfBath, 
    AgeOfHouse = as.numeric(YrSold) - as.numeric(YearBuilt), 
    TotalPorchSF = OpenPorchSF + EnclosedPorch + X3SsnPorch + ScreenPorch,
    TotalFunctionalSF = TotalBsmtSF + X1stFlrSF + X2ndFlrSF
  ) %>%
  #Total Bath is from Bruin AgeOfHouse is mentioned in it too but we also did it in my groups project last semester
  step_ns(LotFrontage, options = list(df = 5)) %>% # natural spline
  step_BoxCox(LotArea) %>%
  ##########PART 1##########
  step_interact(terms = ~ OverallQual:TotalArea) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ YearBuilt:OverallCond) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ OverallQual:Neighborhood) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ LotArea:Neighborhood) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ AgeOfHouse:LotArea) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ AgeOfHouse:YearRemodAdd) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ MSZoning:TotalArea) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ BsmtQual:TotalBsmtSF) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ BsmtExposure:BsmtFinSF1) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ MSZoning:GrLivArea) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ BedroomAbvGr:GrLivArea) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ GarageCars:GrLivArea) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ Neighborhood:OverallQual) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_zv(all_predictors()) %>% # removes variables with a single value
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>% 
  step_nzv(all_predictors()) %>% # will potentially remove variables that are highly sparse and unbalanced.
  step_corr(all_numeric_predictors())
 
sp_recipe_prep <- prep(sp_recipe)
 
 
# 9. Bake the training data
sp_train_baked <- bake(sp_recipe_prep, new_data = sp_train)
 
# 10. Bake the test data
sp_test_baked <- bake(sp_recipe_prep, new_data = sp_test)
```
# Ridge Regression
 
```{r Ridge Regression}
# 11. Model specification (RIDGE REGRESSION - alpha = 0)
sp_glmnet <- parsnip::linear_reg(
  penalty = tune(), 
  mixture = 0 # Ridge regression: mixture = 0
) %>% 
  set_mode("regression") %>%
  set_engine("glmnet") 
 
# 12. Workflow
sp_wf <- workflow() %>% 
  add_recipe(sp_recipe) %>% 
  add_model(sp_glmnet)
 
# 13. 10-fold cross-validation with 5 repeats (using the *original* training data)
sp_kfolds <- vfold_cv(sp_train, v = 10, repeats = 5, strata = "SalePrice")
 
# 14. Tune the model
sp_tune <- tune_grid(
  sp_wf,
  resamples = sp_kfolds,
  grid = 10, # You can adjust the grid size as needed
  metrics = metric_set(rmse, rsq, mae)
)
 
# 15. Display best results
sp_tune %>% show_best(metric = "rmse")
sp_tune %>% show_best(metric = "mae")
 
# Display best results (and get tuning parameter values)
best_rmse <- sp_tune %>% show_best(metric = "rmse")
print("Best RMSE Results:")
print(best_rmse)
 
best_mae <- sp_tune %>% show_best(metric = "mae")
print("Best MAE Results:")
print(best_mae)
 
# 16. Select best tuning parameters
sp_best_tune <- sp_tune %>% select_best(metric = "rmse")
 
# Extract the optimal penalty value
optimal_penalty <- sp_best_tune$penalty
print(paste("Optimal Penalty (lambda):", optimal_penalty))
 
# 17. Finalize workflow
sp_final_workflow <- sp_wf %>% 
  finalize_workflow(sp_best_tune)
 
# 18. Fit the final model to the *original* training data
sp_final_fit <- fit(sp_final_workflow, data = sp_train)
 
# 19. Extract coefficients
sp_coef_Ridge <- sp_final_fit %>%
  fit(sp_train) %>% 
  extract_fit_parsnip() %>%
  tidy()
 
sp_coef_Ridge
 
# 20. Make predictions on the *baked* test data
test_predictions <- predict(sp_final_fit, new_data = sp_test)
 
# 21. Rename the prediction column
test_predictions <- test_predictions %>%
  rename(SalePrice = .pred)
 
# 22. Combine IDs and predictions
test_predictions_with_ids <- tibble(Id = sp_test_ids, SalePrice = test_predictions$SalePrice)
 
# 23. Save predictions with IDs
write_csv(test_predictions_with_ids, "C:\\Users\\jasmi\\OneDrive - Southern Methodist University\\6302\\Data Assignments\\DA1\\test_predictionsRR.csv")
 
```
# LASSO
 
```{r LASSO}
# 11. Model specification (LASSO - alpha = 1)
sp_glmnet <- parsnip::linear_reg(
  penalty = tune(), 
  mixture = 1 # LASSO regression: mixture = 1
) %>% 
  set_mode("regression") %>%
  set_engine("glmnet") 
 
# 12. Workflow
sp_wf <- workflow() %>% 
  add_recipe(sp_recipe) %>% 
  add_model(sp_glmnet)
 
# 13. 10-fold cross-validation with 5 repeats (using the *original* training data)
sp_kfolds <- vfold_cv(sp_train, v = 10, repeats = 5, strata = "SalePrice")
 
# 14. Tune the model
sp_tune <- tune_grid(
  sp_wf,
  resamples = sp_kfolds,
  grid = 10, # You can adjust the grid size as needed
  metrics = metric_set(rmse, rsq, mae)
)
 
# 15. Display best results
sp_tune %>% show_best(metric = "rmse")
sp_tune %>% show_best(metric = "mae")
 
# Display best results (and get tuning parameter values)
best_rmse <- sp_tune %>% show_best(metric = "rmse")
print("Best RMSE Results:")
print(best_rmse)
 
# 16. Select best tuning parameters
sp_best_tune <- sp_tune %>% select_best(metric = "rmse")
 
# 17. Finalize workflow
sp_final_workflow <- sp_wf %>% 
  finalize_workflow(sp_best_tune)
 
# 18. Fit the final model to the *original* training data
sp_final_fit <- fit(sp_final_workflow, data = sp_train)
 
# 19. Extract coefficients
sp_coef_LASSO <- sp_final_fit %>%
  extract_fit_parsnip() %>%
  tidy()
 
sp_coef_LASSO
 
# 20. Make predictions on the *baked* test data
test_predictions <- predict(sp_final_fit, new_data = sp_test)
 
# 21. Rename the prediction column
test_predictions <- test_predictions %>%
  rename(SalePrice = .pred)
 
# 22. Combine IDs and predictions
test_predictions_with_ids <- tibble(Id = sp_test_ids, SalePrice = test_predictions$SalePrice)
 
# 23. Save predictions with IDs
write_csv(test_predictions_with_ids, "C:\\Users\\jasmi\\OneDrive - Southern Methodist University\\6302\\Data Assignments\\DA1\\test_predictionsL.csv")
```

# Elastic Net
 
```{r Elastic Net}
# 11. Model specification (ELASTIC NET REGRESSION)
sp_glmnet <- parsnip::linear_reg(
penalty = tune(),
mixture = tune() # Tune the mixture as well
) %>%
set_mode("regression") %>%
set_engine("glmnet")
 
# 12. Workflow (no changes)
sp_wf <- workflow() %>%
add_recipe(sp_recipe) %>%
add_model(sp_glmnet)
 
# 13. 10-fold cross-validation with 5 repeats
sp_kfolds <- vfold_cv(sp_train, v = 10, repeats = 5, strata = "SalePrice")
 
# 14. Tune the model (important change here!)
sp_tune <- tune_grid(
sp_wf,
resamples = sp_kfolds,
grid = grid_regular(penalty(range = c(-10, 0)), # Example penalty range
         mixture(range = c(0, 1)), # Mixture range 0 to 1
         levels = 10), # Adjust levels as needed
metrics = metric_set(rmse, rsq, mae)
)
 
# 15. Display best results
sp_tune %>% show_best(metric = "rmse")
sp_tune %>% show_best(metric = "mae")
 
# 16. Select best tuning parameters
sp_best_tune <- sp_tune %>% select_best(metric = "rmse")
 
# 17. Finalize workflow
sp_final_workflow <- sp_wf %>% 
  finalize_workflow(sp_best_tune)
 
# 18. Fit the final model to the *original* training data
sp_final_fit <- fit(sp_final_workflow, data = sp_train)
 
# 19. Extract coefficients
sp_coef_EN <- sp_final_fit %>%
  extract_fit_parsnip() %>%
  tidy()
 
sp_coef_EN
 
# 20. Make predictions on the *baked* test data
test_predictions <- predict(sp_final_fit, new_data = sp_test)
 
# 21. Rename the prediction column
test_predictions <- test_predictions %>%
  rename(SalePrice = .pred)
 
# 22. Combine IDs and predictions
test_predictions_with_ids <- tibble(Id = sp_test_ids, SalePrice = test_predictions$SalePrice)
 
# 23. Save predictions with IDs
write_csv(test_predictions_with_ids, "C:\\Users\\jasmi\\OneDrive - Southern Methodist University\\6302\\Data Assignments\\DA1\\test_predictionsEN.csv")
```

# Data Assignment Questions and extras
 
```{r DA Steps}
library(ggplot2)
library(mgcv)
library(caret)
 
#Part 2
nearZeroVar(sp_train)
colnames(sp_train)[nearZeroVar(sp_train)]
 
#Part 3
# Method 2: Using names() and sapply() (base R)
continuous_vars <- names(sp_train)[sapply(sp_train, is.numeric)]
print(continuous_vars)
 
plot_variable <- function(data, variable_name) {
  plot <- ggplot(data, aes(x = .data[[variable_name]], y = SalePrice)) +
    geom_point() +
    labs(title = paste("Scatterplot of SalePrice vs.", variable_name),
         x = variable_name,
         y = "SalePrice") +
    theme_bw()
  print(plot)
}
 
p1 <- plot_variable(sp_train, "LotArea")
p2 <- plot_variable(sp_train, "TotalBsmtSF")
p3 <- plot_variable(sp_train, "GarageArea")
p4 <- plot_variable(sp_train, "EnclosedPorch")
p5 <- plot_variable(sp_train, "PoolArea")
p6 <- plot_variable(sp_train, "GrLivArea")
p7 <- plot_variable(sp_train, "WoodDeckSF")
p8 <- plot_variable(sp_train, "LotFrontage")
p9 <- plot_variable(sp_train, "OpenPorchSF")
p10 <- plot_variable(sp_train, "ScreenPorch")
# LotFrontage seems the least linear
 
library(ggpubr)
 
# Arrange plots in a 3x4 grid
ggarrange(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7, p8, p9, p10,
          ncol = 3, nrow = 4)
 
 
 
# Part 4 D
# --- Variable Tracking and Summary Function ---
track_and_summarize_variables <- function(original_data, baked_data, model_coef, model_name) {
 
  # 1. Original Variables (from sp_train_original)
  original_variables <- names(original_data[, -which(names(original_data) == "SalePrice")])
 
  # 2. Variables After Baking (from sp_train_baked)
  baked_variables <- names(baked_data[, -which(names(baked_data) == "SalePrice")])
 
  # 3. Variables with Coefficients (from model_coef)
  coef_variables <- model_coef$term  # or model_coef %>% pull(term)
 
  # 4. Variables Retained in the Model (coefficients not equal to zero)
  retained_vars <- model_coef %>%
    filter(estimate != 0) %>%
    pull(term)
 
  # 5. Variables Removed During Preprocessing (Baked - Original)
  removed_preprocessing <- setdiff(original_variables, baked_variables)
 
  # 6. Variables Removed by the Model (Baked - Retained)
  removed_model <- setdiff(baked_variables, retained_vars)
 
  cat("\n---", model_name, "Variable Summary ---\n")
  cat("Original Variables:\n")
  print(original_variables)
  cat("\nVariables After Preprocessing (Baked):\n")
  print(baked_variables)
  cat("\nVariables with Coefficients:\n")
  print(coef_variables)
  cat("\nVariables Retained by", model_name, ":\n")
  print(retained_vars)
  cat("\nVariables Removed During Preprocessing:\n")
  print(removed_preprocessing)
  cat("\nVariables Removed by", model_name, ":\n")
  print(removed_model)
  cat("--------------------------------------------\n")
 
    return(list(original = original_variables, baked = baked_variables, coef = coef_variables, retained = retained_vars, removed_preprocessing = removed_preprocessing, removed_model = removed_model))
}
 
# --- Ridge Regression Variable Tracking ---
ridge_summary <- track_and_summarize_variables(sp_train_original, sp_train_baked, sp_coef_Ridge, "Ridge Regression")
 
# --- LASSO Variable Tracking ---
lasso_summary <- track_and_summarize_variables(sp_train_original, sp_train_baked, sp_coef_LASSO, "LASSO") # Replace sp_coef_lasso
 
# --- Elastic Net Variable Tracking ---
en_summary <- track_and_summarize_variables(sp_train_original, sp_train_baked, sp_coef_EN, "Elastic Net") # Replace sp_coef_en
 
 
track_final_model_variables <- function(model_coef, model_name) {
  # Extract variables with nonzero coefficients (final model variables)
  final_vars <- model_coef %>%
    filter(estimate != 0) %>%
    pull(term)
 
  cat("\n---", model_name, "Final Model Variables ---\n")
  print(final_vars)
  cat("--------------------------------------------\n")
 
  return(final_vars)
}
 
# --- Ridge Regression Final Variables ---
ridge_final_vars <- track_final_model_variables(sp_coef_Ridge, "Ridge Regression")
 
# --- LASSO Final Variables ---
lasso_final_vars <- track_final_model_variables(sp_coef_LASSO, "LASSO")
 
# --- Elastic Net Final Variables ---
en_final_vars <- track_final_model_variables(sp_coef_EN, "Elastic Net")
 
 
library(tidymodels)
library(car)  # For influence plot
 
# Extract nonzero coefficients from Elastic Net model
sp_coef_EN <- sp_final_fit %>%
  extract_fit_parsnip() %>%
  tidy()
 
# Get significant predictors (excluding intercept)
important_vars <- sp_coef_EN %>%
  filter(estimate != 0, term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  pull(term)
 
# Create a formula for a linear model using only selected predictors
formula_lm <- as.formula(paste("SalePrice ~", paste(important_vars, collapse = " + ")))
 
# Fit an equivalent linear model (lm) using selected variables
final_model_lm <- lm(formula_lm, data = sp_train_baked)
 
# Influence plot using car package
influencePlot(final_model_lm, id.method = "identify", main = "Influence Plot")
 
# Identify influential points based on Cook's Distance
influential_points <- which(cooks.distance(final_model_lm) > (4 / nrow(sp_train)))
print("Influential Points Identified by Cook's Distance:")
print(influential_points)
 
# Diagnostics: Leverage and studentized residuals
leverage <- hatvalues(final_model_lm)
studentized_residuals <- rstudent(final_model_lm)
 
# Check for high-leverage points
high_leverage_points <- which(leverage > (2 * mean(leverage)))
print("High Leverage Points:")
print(high_leverage_points)
 
####PART 4B ####
library(ggplot2)
library(tidymodels)
library(glmnet)
 
# 1. Convert training data to a matrix format for glmnet
x_train <- as.matrix(select(sp_train_baked, -SalePrice))
y_train <- sp_train_baked$SalePrice
 
# 2. Fit Ridge (alpha = 0), LASSO (alpha = 1), and Elastic Net (alpha = 0.5)
ridge_fit <- glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 0) # Ridge
lasso_fit <- glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 1) # LASSO
en_fit <- glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 0.5)  # Elastic Net
 
# 3. Plot coefficient paths
par(mfrow = c(1, 3))  # Arrange plots in a row
 
plot(ridge_fit, xvar = "lambda", label = TRUE, main = "Ridge Regression Paths")
plot(lasso_fit, xvar = "lambda", label = TRUE, main = "LASSO Regression Paths")
plot(en_fit, xvar = "lambda", label = TRUE, main = "Elastic Net Paths")
 
par(mfrow = c(1,1))  # Reset plotting layout
 
```
